_comment: TEMPLATE — storyboard.json v2 (the single accreting production document; full contract in docs/storyboard_schema.md). Every studio pass (script_analyzer -> film_director -> storyboard_artist -> scene_composer -> asset_planner -> motion_director -> ae_director) fills its own block; the `passes` ledger records the ISO date each finished. This example is fully enriched and shows all three build types: `plate` (scene_01, scene_03), `plate+layers` (scene_02), and `assembly` (scene_04). Asset paths under assets_library/ are illustrative — the asset_planner pass resolves `layers[].asset` against assets_library/INDEX.md. Legacy v1 fields (type / motion / animation_prompt / animated_clip / image_prompt) are retired; see the schema doc's 'Retired v1 fields'.

scene_recipes.establishing: wide isometric establishing view of the full structure in its landscape, warm golden-hour light
scene_recipes.cross_section: clean isometric cutaway, one system revealed, muted diagram guide lines

continuity_registry.light: upper-left, warm golden-hour — every plate, every asset, every scene

characters.water_slave_01: sun-worn tunic, rope belt, carries a wooden tally

continuity_registry.maps: north up, terrain muted, aqueduct route in the terracotta accent

scenes.narration_segment: Two thousand years ago, Roman engineers moved a river across a valley — using nothing but the pull of gravity.
scenes.purpose: hook — the impossible-looking span, before any explanation

composition.frame: wide isometric of the Pont du Gard's three arch tiers spanning the Gardon gorge, golden-hour
composition.focal_point: the topmost water channel, upper third

plate.subject: wide isometric establishing view of the Pont du Gard, three tiers of arches over a river gorge
visual_facts: the largest arches are on the lowest tier
the water channel runs along the very top

ae_build.hierarchy: single plate under a CAMERA_CTRL null; scale-driven push

scenes.narration_segment: Stand beneath it and you feel the scale: the top of the channel is as high as a fifteen-story building.
scenes.purpose: convey scale — a lone human figure and a drifting sky against the stone

composition.frame: isometric of one arch pier rising the full frame, a lone figure at its base
composition.focal_point: the figure at the pier base, lower-left third

plate.subject: isometric of a single Pont du Gard pier rising the full frame height, warm ashlar limestone
visual_facts: ashlar limestone blocks laid without mortar
projecting putlog stones left proud for scaffolding

layers.placement: base of the pier, lower-left, facing right
layers.scale_hint: tiny — read as a silhouette for scale

ae_build.hierarchy: CAMERA_CTRL null > [plate, water_slave, cloud]; cloud parented at 0.25x via slider

scenes.narration_segment: The secret is the slope. Over fifty kilometres the channel drops only a few centimetres every hundred metres — a gradient too fine to see with the eye.
scenes.purpose: explain the mechanism — the near-imperceptible hydraulic gradient

composition.frame: clean isometric cutaway of the covered water channel, slope guide line exaggerated
composition.mid: the stone channel cutaway with flowing water

plate.subject: isometric cutaway of the specus (water channel): stone sides, waterproof opus signinum lining, water flowing left to right
visual_facts: water fills roughly half the channel height

ae_build.hierarchy: plate under CAMERA_CTRL null; label callouts as text layers, staggered fade-in

scenes.narration_segment: To the men who built it there was no formula for any of this. There was only the water — and whether, at the far end, it arrived.
scenes.purpose: narrative beat — the human stake, the witness watching the first flow

composition.frame: flat stage: parchment ground line, the water-slave kneeling at a channel mouth, cypress right
composition.mid: the figure at the channel mouth
composition.bg: flat parchment + a distant arcade silhouette

layers.placement: left third, kneeling at the channel mouth, facing right

motion.note: hand dips toward the water at t=3s, 2-frame anticipation

ae_build.hierarchy: flat stage, no camera null needed; figure + cypress as siblings
